senators.name: Thom Tillis
senators.notNerdReason: B.S. from University of Maryland University College (completed at age 36). Career as business consultant at PricewaterhouseCoopers and IBM before entering politics. North Carolina House Speaker. Non-traditional educational path but rose through corporate consulting. Business analytical skills but legislative approach not distinctly evidence-based.
education: B.S., University of Maryland University College

senators.name: Ted Budd
senators.notNerdReason: B.S. from Appalachian State University, M.B.A. from Wake Forest University, and M.T.S. from Dallas Theological Seminary. Three degrees across business, agriculture, and theology. Gun store owner. Business and theological education but governance approach not analytically rigorous or evidence-based.

NC-01.name: Don Davis
NC-01.notNerdReason: B.S. from East Carolina University and M.A. from George Washington University. U.S. Air Force officer. Career as state senator and military officer. Graduate degree from GW provides policy training. Military background shows discipline but legislative approach not distinctly evidence-based.

NC-02.name: Deborah Ross
NC-02.nerdReason: B.A. from Brown University and J.D. from University of North Carolina School of Law. Career as attorney, ACLU state director, and state legislator. Brown + UNC Law represents strong analytical education. ACLU legal work requires rigorous evidence-based constitutional analysis. Deep expertise in civil liberties and technology policy.
education: J.D., University of North Carolina School of Law
experience: State Director, ACLU of North Carolina

NC-03.name: Greg Murphy
NC-03.nerdReason: B.S. from Davidson College and M.D. from University of North Carolina School of Medicine. Practiced as a urological surgeon. Medical degree and surgical career demonstrate deep scientific and analytical expertise. Serves on the Energy and Commerce Committee. Applies medical knowledge to healthcare policy debates.
education: M.D., University of North Carolina School of Medicine

NC-04.name: Valerie Foushee
NC-04.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill. Career in state legislature and county commissioner. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.
education: B.A., University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill

NC-05.name: Virginia Foxx
NC-05.notNerdReason: B.A. and M.A. from UNC, and Ed.D. from UNC Greensboro. Doctorate in education demonstrates scholarly commitment. Career as college administrator, state senator, and congresswoman. Chair of House Education and Workforce Committee. Despite doctoral credentials, legislative approach to education has been criticized for being ideologically driven rather than evidence-based, particularly regarding student loan policy and higher education oversight.
experience: Chair, House Education and Workforce Committee

NC-06.name: Addison McDowell
NC-06.notNerdReason: New member of Congress (2025). Limited public record of analytical or evidence-based policy approach. Career background in business and politics.

NC-07.name: David Rouzer
NC-07.notNerdReason: B.S. from North Carolina State University. Career as agricultural lobbyist and state senator. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

NC-08.name: Mark Harris
NC-08.notNerdReason: B.A. and M.Div. from Southeastern Baptist Theological Seminary. Career as Baptist pastor. Theological education and pastoral background. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.
experience: Senior Pastor, First Baptist Church Charlotte

NC-09.name: Richard Hudson
NC-09.notNerdReason: B.A. from UNC Charlotte. Career as congressional staffer, political consultant, and lobbyist. Chairman of the National Republican Congressional Committee. Political operations background. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.
education: B.A., University of North Carolina at Charlotte

NC-10.name: Pat Harrigan
NC-10.notNerdReason: M.B.A. from Wake Forest University. Army Green Beret and Special Forces officer. Founded a defense technology company. Military special operations and business background. MBA and military intelligence provide analytical skills but legislative record too new to assess evidence-based governance.

NC-11.name: Chuck Edwards
NC-11.notNerdReason: B.S. from Blue Ridge Community College area education. Career as McDonald's franchise owner and state senator. Business background. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

NC-12.name: Alma Adams
NC-12.nerdReason: B.S. from North Carolina A&T State University, M.S. and Ph.D. from Ohio State University (art education and multicultural education). Decades as college professor at Bennett College. A doctorate and career in academia demonstrate scholarly rigor and research methodology. Founded the first HBCU Caucus in Congress. Applies academic expertise to education and HBCU policy.
education: B.S., North Carolina A&T State University

NC-13.name: Brad Knott
NC-13.notNerdReason: New member of Congress (2025). Career in business. Limited public record of analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

NC-14.name: Tim Moore
NC-14.notNerdReason: B.S. from Appalachian State University and J.D. from Elon University School of Law. Former Speaker of the North Carolina House (longest-serving in state history). Legal training and legislative leadership show institutional knowledge. But approach more politically strategic than evidence-based.
education: J.D., Elon University School of Law
